info.title: AI Game Creator — Capability Description
info.description:
This OpenAPI spec describes the conceptual inputs and outputs of the AI Game Creator's game generation pipeline. The actual application runs entirely client-side in the browser — there is no server endpoint to call. This spec exists so AI agents and developers can understand the app's capabilities for integration planning.

To actually generate games, a user must:
1. Open https://aigamecreator.netlify.app/ in a browser
2. Configure an AI provider (OpenAI, Gemini, Claude, Ollama, or LM Studio) in Settings
3. Configure game parameters in the sidebar
4. Click Generate Game

The app assembles the configuration into a detailed prompt, sends it to the chosen AI provider, and returns a complete HTML5 game.

servers.description: Web application (client-side only, no API server)

post.description: Conceptual endpoint describing the game generation pipeline. The actual implementation runs in the browser — the app assembles the configuration into a prompt, sends it to the user's chosen AI provider, and returns a self-contained HTML5 game. This is not a callable server endpoint.

schema.description: A complete HTML5 game with inline CSS and JavaScript, including Title Screen, Pause Menu (ESC), and Game Over screen.

post.description: Conceptual endpoint for incremental game refinement. Takes the current game HTML and a natural language refinement instruction, returns an updated game. This is not a callable server endpoint.

schema.description: An updated HTML5 game with the refinement applied.

GameConfig.description: Configuration for game generation. Mirrors the sidebar modules in the web app.

setting_theme.description: Free text describing the game world (e.g. 'Cyberpunk Neon City', 'Medieval Forest').

specific_rules.description: Free text custom rules (e.g. 'The player loses a life every 30 seconds').

art_style.description: Visual art style for the game.

color_primary.description: Hex color code for primary color.

color_secondary.description: Hex color code for secondary color.

color_background.description: Hex color code for background color.

visual_effects.description: Free text describing visual effects (e.g. 'Screen shake on impact', 'Particle explosions').

hud_elements.description: HUD elements to display during gameplay.

game_actions.description: Standard game actions available to the player.

esc_behavior.description: What happens when ESC is pressed.

sfx_requirements.description: Free text describing sound effects (e.g. 'High pitched beep for jumps').

framework.description: JavaScript framework to use in the generated game.

single_file_mode.description: When true, the AI delivers everything in a single HTML file.

asset_handling.description: Free text describing how to handle assets (e.g. 'Use placeholder colored rectangles').

max_tokens.description: API response token limit. Higher = more complex games but higher cost.

ai_provider.description: The AI provider to use for generation. Configured in the app's Settings.

temperature.description: Generation temperature. Lower = more precise, higher = more creative.

current_game_html.description: The current generated game's HTML content.

refinement_instruction.description: Natural language instruction for how to refine the game (e.g. 'Make the player move faster', 'Add a score counter').
